title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. atlantica
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. atlantica, also known as the Atlantic lady's mantle, is a subspecies of the common lady's mantle plant, characterized by its low-growing, spreading habit and glossy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and astringent effects, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. atlantica include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea and dyspepsia. The herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and creams, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
